q: A camera is an optical instrument that forms and records an ___ of an object.
In a camera, a ___ focuses the light from the object to form a real inverted image.
Which component is placed behind the convex lens to control the amount of light energy falling on the film?
The ___ is a circular opening through which the light passes.
The $f$-number of a camera is the ratio of the ___ of the lens to the diameter of aperture.
When taking a photograph in bright light, a ___ $f$-number must be used.
A ___ controls the time during which light enters the camera through the lens.
The shutter speed is expressed in terms of the ___.
The shorter the exposure time, the ___ the shutter speed is.
The ___ consists of a thin layer of chemical compounds of silver.
In a digital camera, the electronic sensor is made up of millions of tiny ___ which respond to light.
The smaller the $f$-number, the ___ the aperture is.
When taking a photograph in dim light, a ___ $f$-number (wide aperture) must be used.
Which part of the camera allows the distance between the lens and film to be adjusted for a sharp image?
